rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Answer
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 19, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANTS DAVID ASHER, MARIANNE ASHER'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Answer Hearing Required. No later than 9:00 a.m. on March 20, 2019 Defendant to file and serve a supplemental brief responding to the following questions (no further argument is allowed; if any non-responsive material is added, the supplemental brief will be stricken in its entirety): 1. Identify by page and line number and quote parts of the Cross-Complaint asserting "irrevocable license." List all elements of "irrevocable license" with authority, e.g. case, statute, CACI. If no such claims appear in the Cross-Complaint, state "none." 2. Does Defendant need any further discovery as to (1) equitable easement; and (2) irrevocable license? If yes, identify the discovery, e.g. 30 Special Interrogatories, Disposition of person X, etc. If no, state "none." 3. Does Defendant have experts on the affirmative defenses at issue? If yes, identify how many and specify the dates their reports will be available and the dates they will be available for deposition. If no, state "none." = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. MICHAEL E. JOHNSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 19, 2019 line 3. Demurrer To First Amended Cross-Complaint sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for breach of contract. City is not a party to the contract and Plaintiff's theories attempting to establish that the City is a party are not supported by law or fact. Plaintiff failed to indicate what facts may be alleged to show that the City is a party to the action. To the extent Plaintiff is seeking a leave to amend (18 lines on page 11) to add new causes of action, the Court will not rule on that request made in opposition. Plaintiff shall file a property motion for leave to amend. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: WILMINGTON SAVINGS FUND SOCIETY, FSB VS. EMIL P MILYAKOV ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 19, 2019 line 5. DEFENDANT MAGDALENA APOSTOLOVA Mtn To Vacate Unlawful Detainer DENIED. This motion is not accompanied by any evidence. The first basis for the motion is alleged inconstancy between the property being described by Defendant as a single family dwelling and Plaintiff seeking possession of a "Lower Unit." Legal grounds for seeking to vacate judgment entered on September 24, 2018 on this basis are not asserted. The second basis for the motion is extrinsic fraud. Defendant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that asking for a trial in a summary proceeding constitutes fraud or that Defendant could make herself unavailable during an unlawful detainer action from August 12 to October 2. Defendant further fails to explain why she did not request the trial to be set prior to her extended unavailability. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: WILMINGTON SAVINGS FUND SOCIETY, FSB, DBA VS. EMIL P. MILYAKOV ET AL

rulings.Case Title: EQUITY GROWTH ASSET MANAGEMENT VS. LILLIE B HART ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 19, 2019 line 7. DEFENDANT MICHAEL WILLIAMS, DEBORAH WILLIAMS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendant Michael Williams Demurrer is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer under CCP 1161a. Defendant improper raises factual issues on the demurrer. Defendant Debra Williams' Demurrer is OFF CALENDAR. Notice of Bankruptcy stay on file. = (501/REQ)
